attributes.content:
## Overview

The Expense Report card gives the workspace a structured home for collection of expenses with receipts, categories, approval workflow, and reimbursement status.

As a first-class Boxel card, it can be linked to adjacent records, surfaced in filtered views, and reused in automation without the information collapsing into disconnected notes or one-off documents.

## Core Mechanics

Expense Report acts as a canonical operational record. It keeps important facts, state changes, timestamps, and linked parties in one place so downstream workflows, approvals, and reporting can all rely on the same source of truth.

The card can be created directly by a user, produced from a [[Workflow Playbook]], or updated in response to a [[Trigger]]. When AI is involved, a [[Prompt Template]] or [[Skill Card]] can shape how it is drafted, summarized, classified, or routed.

## Integration Points

- **[[Invoice]]** -- Invoice can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Expense Report.
- **[[Order]]** -- Order can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Expense Report.
- **[[Deal]]** -- Deal can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Expense Report.
- **[[Report]]** -- Expense Report can feed rollups and summaries that surface trends, status, or outcomes.
- **[[Metrics (KPI)]]** -- Metrics (KPI) can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Expense Report.
- **[[Workflow Playbook]]** -- Expense Report can be created, reviewed, or progressed as one step inside a repeatable workflow.

## Example Use Cases

- **System of record:** Teams create Expense Report as the structured home for collection of expenses with receipts, categories, approval workflow, and reimbursement status and keep related work linked instead of scattered across notes.
- **Automation handoff:** A [[Workflow Playbook]] or [[Trigger]] creates or updates Expense Report when upstream conditions are met, reducing repetitive setup work.
- **Search and reporting:** Expense Report stays queryable for dashboards, summaries, and downstream actions without re-entering the same information in multiple places.

cardInfo.summary: Collection of expenses with receipts, categories, approval workflow, and reimbursement status.
